input.background: Refer to Example 11.1 and the simple linear regression of the advertising-sales data in Table 11.1.
input.data:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline Table 11.1 & Advertising-Sales Data & \
\hline Month & Advertising Expenditure, $x(\$ 100 \mathrm{~s})$ & Sales Revenue, $y$ ( $\$ 1,000 \mathrm{~s}$ ) \
\hline 1 & 1 & 1 \
\hline 2 & 2 & 1 \
\hline 3 & 3 & 2 \
\hline 4 & 4 & 2 \
\hline 5 & 5 & 4 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: Compute an estimate of σ.

output.answer:
We previously calculated $mathrm{SSE}=1.10$ for the least squares line $hat{y}=-.1+.7 x$. Recalling that there were $n=5$ data points, we have $n-2=5-2=3 mathrm{df}$ for estimating $sigma^2$. Thus,

$$
s^2=frac{ext { SSE }}{n-2}=frac{1.10}{3}=.367
$$

is the estimated variance, and

$$
s=sqrt{.367}=.61
$$

is the standard error of the regression model.
